method_atom: A precision-measurement argument that derives an irreducible indeterminacy in the relative position of events from a fundamental lower bound on how finely position can be resolved by a probe, then predicts, with no free parameters, a stochastic departure from a classically defined reference configuration; this fluctuation accumulates as a random walk of phase with a flat (white) power spectral density and a distinctive directional signature that appears only when comparing displacements transverse to the probe direction and vanishes for purely longitudinal ones. The prediction is bounded from below by a covariant entropy limit and cross-checked against the residual noise of an operating comparison instrument.
object_atom: The quantum fluctuations of spacetime geometry — the relative spacetime positions of reflection events — probed with Planck-wavelength radiation and constrained by gravitational entropy, as manifested in the noise of laser interferometers such as GEO600 and LIGO where the effect competes with gravitational-wave signals.
brief_reason: The resolution-bound-to-parameter-free-fluctuation-spectrum machinery, with its transverse-only random-walk phase signature, is the transferable method, while the spacetime geometry probed by Planck-scale interferometry is the physics-specific object.
